rows.notes: Centered page title in Latin. Handwriting clear though the exact Latin spelling has a minor uncertainty (praesentis vs praesens). No amounts on this line.
Description legible as place/date. Pounds (68), shillings (17) and pence (6) are well aligned to the three ruling columns on the right and all values fall within valid ranges.
Description clear. Amount reads as 02 (pounds) 15s 0d; alignment with the £/s/d lines is unambiguous and shillings/pence are valid.

Amount 30-1-11 is clearly written and aligns with the columns. Description is partially uncertain: appears to read 'Longittham Recd. ad Sept. Annu.' (some letters unclear), so transcription of the place/abbrev is tentative.

rows.description: Mrs Blyth 1/2 years annuity to Lady day
rows.notes: Description (Mrs Blyth half years annuity to Lady day) is readable. Amount 2-10-0 is clearly aligned and digits are unambiguous.
Centered section heading 'Recepta Varia' (various receipts). Clear with no monetary figures on the same line.

Pounds/shillings/pence (30-0-0) are clear and align well. Description is uncertain: first word 'Little' is clear but second word could be 'Sew', 'Sea' or 'Sow' and the next word likely 'Timber' (t resembles an S in the hand). I transcribed as 'Little Sew Timber Money' acknowledging ambiguity in the middle word.

rows.description: Money pd wrongfully on acct of timber expences
rows.notes: Amount 1-16-0 is clear. Description reads as 'Money pd wrongfully on acct of timber expences' — 'wrongfully' and 'expences' (spelling) visible; alignment supports this reading.

rows.description: Fee returned by Mr Wright junr & forgot to be entred
rows.notes: Amount 1-1-0 is legible and columns are well aligned. Description reads as 'fee returned by Mr Wright Junr & forgot to be entred' — 'Junr' and 'entred' are clear in the handwriting, minor abbreviations present.
Amount 100-0-0 is unambiguous. Description reads as 'for Shortridge's Benefaction due Mid. 1758' (Mid. = Michaelmas/Mids?; 'Shortridge' spelling observed in the ledger hand).
Amount 100-0-0 clear. Description similar to previous line; final word looks like '1758' and the preceding word reads like 'Kings' or 'King's' (slight ambiguity in that particular word).

A small subtotal appears in the rightmost columns reading 337-1-5. Numbers are legible though cramped; no label immediately to the left of these numerals making the exact grouping they total a little uncertain.

Second subtotal reads 348-19-8. Digits are clearer here; shillings at 19 and pence 8 are unambiguous and within valid ranges. The ledger shows a horizontal line beneath this value, indicating a further aggregation.
Label above the final total. Handwriting clear; Latin phrase indicates the grand sum of receipts. No monetary figures on the same line.
Final grand total boxed/underlined in rightmost columns reads 686-1-1. Numerals are well-formed and aligned with the £/s/d ruling; values validate correctly (shillings <20, pence <12).
